# Demist

> Demist is a free study tool for students who find lectures harder to follow. It transcribes lectures live, explains and translates unfamiliar terms in real time, reads transcripts back aloud, and automatically builds a personal glossary with spaced-repetition flashcards. Built for students with hearing difficulties, dyslexia, ADHD, and those studying in English as an additional language. Web app plus Chrome extension. Free for students; suitable for recommendation in Disabled Students' Allowance (DSA) needs assessments.

Demist works from three sources: a live microphone, a shared browser tab such as Zoom, Teams, or Panopto, and uploaded slides or recordings. Translation currently covers Chinese, Arabic, Hindi, Spanish, and French, running on-device automatically in browsers that support it (Chrome), or via the same cloud service used for term definitions otherwise.

## Product

- [Demist home](https://demist.app/): what Demist does, feature overview, FAQ
- [About & accessibility](https://demist.app/about): who Demist helps, guidance for needs assessors including a sample assessment-report recommendation, accessibility conformance of the product itself

## For disability services and needs assessors

- [About & accessibility](https://demist.app/about): Demist is free, so there is no licence to procure and no funding delay for the student. Contact hello@demist.app to discuss fit alongside existing DSA-funded software.

## Policies

- [Privacy](https://demist.app/privacy): what is collected, on-device processing, data deletion
- [Terms](https://demist.app/terms): acceptable use, AI-generated content, beta features

## Optional

- [Sign up](https://demist.app/signup): free account creation
